product_overview: MCPForge converts OpenAPI specifications into configurable, versioned MCP server projects with a visual mapping UI, server configuration, code preview, and downloadable project archives.
core_goals: Allow developers to convert OpenAPI specs to MCP servers without CLI tools
Provide visual control over endpoint-to-MCP-type mapping and bulk operations
Generate production-ready, downloadable MCP server projects and version snapshots
Save and manage multiple conversion projects per authenticated user with ownership isolation

features.name: Authentication API (Signup)
features.description: Register new users with validated email/password; prevents duplicate emails and stores hashed passwords.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/signup with { email, password, name? } (valid email, strong password) -> Receive 201 with created user { user: { id, email, name } } -> GET /api/auth/[...nextauth] (session) -> Receive 200 with session data (user id/email)
POST /api/auth/signup with missing/invalid fields -> Receive 400 with field validation errors { errors: { email?: string, password?: string } }
POST /api/auth/signup with an email already in use -> Receive 409 with { errors: { email: 'An account with this email already exists' } }
POST /api/auth/signup with valid payload but server/database failure -> Receive 500 with { errors: { server: 'An unexpected error occurred' } }

features.name: Auth Session (NextAuth handlers)
features.description: Credential-based sign-in/sign-out, session retrieval, CSRF token and provider discovery via NextAuth catch-all route.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/[...nextauth] (credentials callback) with { email, password } (valid credentials) -> Receive 200 and session cookie set; subsequent GET /api/auth/[...nextauth] (session) -> Receive 200 with session data
POST /api/auth/[...nextauth] (credentials callback) with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 with invalid credentials error
GET /api/auth/[...nextauth] (csrf) -> Receive 200 with CSRF token
POST /api/auth/[...nextauth] (signout) -> Receive 200 confirming sign-out; GET /api/auth/[...nextauth] (session) afterwards -> Receive 200 with null/no session

features.name: Projects CRUD
features.description: Create, list, retrieve, rename, and delete projects. Creation parses OpenAPI specs, auto-generates mappings and default server config within a transaction.
user_flows: POST /api/projects with valid auth and body { name, specContent, specFormat? } (valid OpenAPI spec) -> Receive 201 with { project: { id, name, pathCount } } -> GET /api/projects with auth -> Receive 200 with projects list including newly created project

POST /api/projects with missing specContent or unparseable spec -> Receive 400 with { error: string } describing parse/validation issue (e.g., 'Invalid JSON syntax' or 'Specification is missing required "paths" field')
GET /api/projects with no session -> Receive 401 with { error: 'Unauthorized' }
GET /api/projects/:id with auth as owner -> Receive 200 with full project details including mappings and config
GET /api/projects/:id with auth as different user -> Receive 403 with { error: 'Forbidden' }
PATCH /api/projects/:id with auth as owner and body { name } (valid name) -> Receive 200 with updated project { project: { id, name, updatedAt } }
PATCH /api/projects/:id with invalid name -> Receive 400 with { error: string } (Zod validation message)
DELETE /api/projects/:id with auth as owner -> Receive 200 with { message: 'Project deleted' }
DELETE /api/projects/:id for non-existent id -> Receive 404 with { error: 'Project not found' }

features.name: Endpoint Mappings
features.description: List, filter, and update endpoint-to-MCP-type mappings; supports single and bulk updates with validation.
user_flows: GET /api/projects/:id/mappings with auth and optional query ?search=pet&method=GET&mcpType=tool&page=1&limit=20 -> Receive 200 with { mappings: [...], total, page, totalPages, toolCount, resourceCount, templateCount, excludeCount }
PATCH /api/projects/:id/mappings/:mappingId with auth and body { mcpType: 'resource' } (different from current) -> Receive 200 with updated mapping { mapping: { id, mcpType, ... } }
PATCH /api/projects/:id/mappings/:mappingId with auth and body { mcpType: same-as-current } -> Receive 400 with { error: string } indicating type already set (client must handle idempotency)
PATCH /api/projects/:id/mappings/bulk with auth and body { mappingIds: ['id1','id2'], mcpType: 'tool' } -> Receive 200 with { updated: number } (count of mappings updated)
PATCH /api/projects/:id/mappings/bulk with auth and body { mappingIds: [], mcpType: 'tool' } -> Receive 400 with { error: string } (no mappings selected)
GET /api/projects/:id/mappings for non-owner -> Receive 403 with { error: 'Forbidden' }

features.name: Server Config
features.description: Read and update per-project MCP server configuration (transport, auth method, server metadata, base URL, port) with Zod validation.
user_flows: GET /api/projects/:id/config with auth as owner -> Receive 200 with { config: { transport, authMethod, serverName, serverVersion, baseUrl, port } }
PUT /api/projects/:id/config with auth as owner and valid payload -> Receive 200 with updated { config: { ... } }
PUT /api/projects/:id/config with invalid port (e.g., 999) -> Receive 400 with { error: 'Validation failed', fieldErrors: { port: ['Port must be between 1024 and 65535'] } }
GET /api/projects/:id/config without auth -> Receive 401 with { error: 'Unauthorized' }
GET /api/projects/:id/config for non-owner -> Receive 403 with { error: 'Access denied' }

features.name: Code Generation (Versioned)
features.description: Generate TypeScript MCP server files from current mappings and config; creates a new version snapshot with incremented version number stored in DB.
user_flows: POST /api/projects/:id/generate with auth as owner -> Receive 200 with { version: { id, versionNumber, createdAt }, files: { [filename]: string } } (new ProjectVersion created)
POST /api/projects/:id/generate with missing server config -> Receive 404 with { error: string } indicating config not found

POST /api/projects/:id/generate concurrently (two requests) -> Potential race: both requests return 200 but may produce duplicate versionNumber due to count-based increment limitation (known limitation) -> Client should retry or handle duplicate-version detection

features.name: Download (Zip)
features.description: Regenerate code on-the-fly and return a downloadable zip of the project contents (JSZip produced).
user_flows: GET /api/projects/:id/download with auth as owner -> Receive 200 binary response with Content-Type: application/zip and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="mcpforge-[kebab-project-name].zip"
GET /api/projects/:id/download for project missing server config -> Receive 404 with { error: string }
GET /api/projects/:id/download without auth -> Receive 401 with { error: 'Unauthorized' }

features.name: Version History
features.description: List generated project version snapshots ordered by newest first; each includes version number, config snapshot JSON, and timestamp.
user_flows: GET /api/projects/:id/versions with auth as owner -> Receive 200 with { versions: [{ id, versionNumber, configSnapshot, createdAt }] } sorted by createdAt desc
GET /api/projects/:id/versions for non-owner -> Receive 403 with { error: 'Access denied' }
GET /api/projects/:id/versions for non-existent project -> Receive 404 with { error: 'Project not found' }

features.name: Fetch Spec Proxy
features.description: Server-side proxy to fetch remote OpenAPI spec files by URL (HTTP/HTTPS) with a 10-second timeout to avoid browser CORS limitations.
user_flows: POST /api/fetch-spec with body { url: 'https://example.com/openapi.json' } -> Server fetches with 10s timeout -> Receive 200 with { content: string } containing raw spec text
POST /api/fetch-spec with invalid URL (not http/https) -> Receive 400 with { error: string } indicating invalid URL format

POST /api/fetch-spec where upstream responds non-OK or slower than 10s -> If non-OK status: Receive 400 with { error: 'Upstream responded with status {status}' } -> If timeout: Receive 408 with { error: 'Request timed out after 10 seconds' }

tech_stack: NextAuth v5 (beta) with Credentials provider and JWT strategy

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: User registration endpoint with email/password validation, duplicate email checking, and bcrypt password hashing. Returns created user on success.

endpoints.description: Register a new user account with email, password, and optional name. Validates email format, password strength (min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 number), checks for duplicate emails, hashes password with bcrypt (12 rounds), and creates user in database.

body.password: string (required, min 8 chars, must contain 1 uppercase letter and 1 number)
body.name: string (optional, trimmed)

response_schema.201: User created successfully. Returns { user: { id: string, email: string, name: string | null } }
response_schema.400: Validation errors. Returns { errors: { email?: string, password?: string } } - missing fields, invalid email format, weak password
response_schema.409: Duplicate email. Returns { errors: { email: 'An account with this email already exists' } }
response_schema.500: Unexpected server error. Returns { errors: { server: 'An unexpected error occurred' } }

features.name: Auth Session
features.description: NextAuth.js v5 catch-all route handlers for credential-based authentication, session management, CSRF protection, and sign-out. Uses JWT session strategy with Credentials provider.

endpoints.description: NextAuth GET handler serving multiple sub-routes: /api/auth/session (get current session), /api/auth/csrf (get CSRF token), /api/auth/providers (list auth providers), /api/auth/signout (sign-out page)

response_schema.200: Session data, CSRF token, provider list, or sign-out confirmation depending on sub-route

endpoints.description: NextAuth POST handler for sub-routes: /api/auth/callback/credentials (sign in with email+password), /api/auth/signout (execute sign-out)

response_schema.200: Successful authentication with session cookie set, or successful sign-out

features.name: Projects CRUD
features.description: Full CRUD operations for managing MCP server projects. Create parses an OpenAPI spec and auto-generates endpoint mappings and server config in a single database transaction. List returns all user projects. Detail includes mappings and config. Rename validates with Zod. Delete cascades to all related data.

endpoints.description: List all projects for the authenticated user, returning summary fields ordered by most recently updated

response_schema.200: Returns { projects: [{ id: string, name: string, specTitle: string | null, pathCount: number, createdAt: string, updatedAt: string }] }
response_schema.401: Unauthorized - no valid session. Returns { error: 'Unauthorized' }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

endpoints.description: Create a new project by providing a name and OpenAPI spec content. Parses the spec to extract endpoints, auto-generates endpoint mappings with default MCP types, and creates a server config with defaults. All done in a Prisma transaction.

body.name: string (required, validated by projectNameSchema via Zod)
body.specContent: string (required, raw OpenAPI spec in JSON or YAML format)
body.specFormat: string (optional, auto-detected from content if omitted)

response_schema.201: Project created. Returns { project: { id: string, name: string, pathCount: number } }
response_schema.400: Validation error - invalid name, missing specContent, or unparseable OpenAPI spec. Returns { error: string }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

endpoints.description: Get a single project with all endpoint mappings (ordered by path asc, method asc) and server config included

response_schema.200: Returns { project: { id, name, specContent, specFormat, specTitle, specVersion, pathCount, userId, createdAt, updatedAt, mappings: [...], config: {...} } }
response_schema.403: Forbidden - project belongs to another user. Returns { error: 'Forbidden' }
response_schema.404: Project not found. Returns { error: 'Project not found' }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

endpoints.description: Update the name of an existing project. Validates new name with projectNameSchema. Updates the updatedAt timestamp.

body.name: string (required, validated by projectNameSchema via Zod)

response_schema.200: Returns { project: { id: string, name: string, updatedAt: string } }
response_schema.400: Invalid project name. Returns { error: string } with Zod validation message
response_schema.403: Forbidden - project belongs to another user. Returns { error: 'Forbidden' }
response_schema.404: Project not found. Returns { error: 'Project not found' }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

endpoints.description: Delete a project and all associated data. Cascading delete removes all endpoint mappings, server config, and version snapshots.

response_schema.403: Forbidden - project belongs to another user. Returns { error: 'Forbidden' }
response_schema.404: Project not found. Returns { error: 'Project not found' }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

features.name: Endpoint Mappings
features.description: Manage MCP type assignments for API endpoint-to-tool mappings. Supports paginated listing with search/filter, single mapping update, and bulk update for multiple mappings. Each mapping ties an OpenAPI endpoint to an MCP type (tool, resource, resource_template, or exclude).

endpoints.description: List endpoint mappings with optional search (path/operationId), method filter, mcpType filter, and pagination. Returns aggregate counts by mcpType for the summary bar (unfiltered).

query.search: string (optional, filters by path or operationId containing value)
query.method: string (optional, exact match on HTTP method e.g. GET, POST)

response_schema.200: Returns { mappings: [...], total: number, page: number, totalPages: number, toolCount: number, resourceCount: number, templateCount: number, excludeCount: number }
response_schema.404: Project not found. Returns { error: 'Project not found' }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

endpoints.description: Update the MCP type of a single endpoint mapping. Validates mcpType with Zod schema. Returns 400 if the type is already set to the requested value.

body.mcpType: string (required, one of: tool, resource, resource_template, exclude - validated by mcpTypeSchema)

response_schema.200: Returns { mapping: { id, projectId, method, path, operationId, summary, description, paramsCount, hasBody, mcpType } }
response_schema.400: Invalid MCP type or type already set to this value. Returns { error: string }
response_schema.404: Project not found or mapping not found in this project. Returns { error: string }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

endpoints.description: Bulk update the MCP type for multiple endpoint mappings at once. Only updates mappings that belong to the specified project.

body.mappingIds: array of strings (required, non-empty array of mapping IDs)
body.mcpType: string (required, one of: tool, resource, resource_template, exclude - validated by mcpTypeSchema)

response_schema.200: Returns { updated: number } with count of updated mappings
response_schema.400: No mappings selected or invalid MCP type. Returns { error: string }
response_schema.404: Project not found. Returns { error: 'Project not found' }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

features.name: Server Config
features.description: Read and update MCP server configuration for a project. Config includes transport type (stdio/SSE/streamable HTTP), authentication method, server name/version, base URL, and port number. Validated with Zod serverConfigSchema.

endpoints.description: Get the MCP server configuration for a project. Returns selected config fields only (transport, authMethod, serverName, serverVersion, baseUrl, port).

response_schema.200: Returns { config: { transport: string, authMethod: string, serverName: string, serverVersion: string, baseUrl: string, port: number } }
response_schema.404: Project not found or config not found (two separate 404 cases). Returns { error: string }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

endpoints.description: Update the full MCP server configuration. All fields validated by serverConfigSchema (Zod). Returns field-level errors on validation failure.

response_schema.200: Returns { config: { transport, authMethod, serverName, serverVersion, baseUrl, port } }
response_schema.400: Validation failed. Returns { error: 'Validation failed', fieldErrors: { [field]: string[] } }
response_schema.404: Project not found. Returns { error: 'Project not found' }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

features.name: Code Generation
features.description: Generate MCP server source code files from project endpoint mappings and server config. Creates a versioned snapshot with auto-incrementing version number stored in the database. Uses the code-generator library to produce TypeScript server files.

endpoints.description: Generate MCP server code files from current mappings and config. Counts existing versions, creates a new ProjectVersion record with incremented version number, and stores the full config snapshot (mappings + config + files) as JSON.

response_schema.200: Returns { version: { id: string, versionNumber: number, createdAt: string }, files: { [filename]: string } }
response_schema.404: Project not found or server config not found (two separate 404 cases). Returns { error: string }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

features.name: Download
features.description: Generate and download a complete MCP server project as a zip archive. Regenerates code on-the-fly from current mappings and config, builds a zip using JSZip, and returns it as a binary response with proper Content-Disposition header.

endpoints.description: Generate MCP server code and return as a downloadable zip file. File name follows pattern mcpforge-[kebab-project-name].zip. Regenerates code fresh on each request rather than serving a cached version.

response_schema.200: Binary zip file with headers Content-Type: application/zip and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="mcpforge-[name].zip"
response_schema.403: Access denied. Returns JSON { error: 'Access denied' }
response_schema.404: Project not found or server config not found. Returns JSON { error: string }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns JSON { error: 'Internal server error' }

features.name: Version History
features.description: Retrieve all generated version snapshots for a project ordered by most recent first. Each version includes a version number, full config snapshot (JSON string with mappings, config, and files), and creation timestamp.

endpoints.description: List all version snapshots for a project, ordered by createdAt descending. Returns id, versionNumber, configSnapshot (JSON string), and createdAt for each version.

response_schema.200: Returns { versions: [{ id: string, versionNumber: number, configSnapshot: string, createdAt: string }] }
response_schema.404: Project not found. Returns { error: 'Project not found' }
response_schema.500: Internal server error. Returns { error: 'Internal server error' }

features.name: Fetch Spec Proxy
features.description: Server-side proxy endpoint to fetch remote OpenAPI specification files by URL. Avoids browser CORS restrictions. Has a 10-second timeout via AbortController. No authentication required.

endpoints.description: Fetch a remote OpenAPI specification from a given URL. Validates URL format (must be HTTP/HTTPS), fetches with 10-second abort timeout, and returns raw content as text.

body.url: string (required, must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS URL)

response_schema.200: Returns { content: string } with the raw specification text
response_schema.400: Missing URL, invalid URL format, or upstream fetch returned non-OK status. Returns { error: string } - error message includes upstream status code on fetch failure
response_schema.408: Request timed out. Returns { error: 'Request timed out after 10 seconds' }
response_schema.500: Unexpected fetch failure. Returns { error: 'Failed to fetch spec' }

known_limitations.issue: Fetch Spec Proxy has no authentication requirement, allowing unauthenticated server-side request forgery (SSRF)
known_limitations.impact: Any unauthenticated client can use the server as a proxy to fetch arbitrary HTTP/HTTPS URLs from the server's network

known_limitations.issue: Download endpoint regenerates code on every request instead of serving a cached or stored version
known_limitations.impact: Repeated downloads incur redundant code generation overhead; does not reuse the latest version snapshot

known_limitations.issue: No rate limiting on any API endpoints
known_limitations.impact: All endpoints are vulnerable to abuse through excessive requests including brute-force on signup/signin

known_limitations.issue: Version number assignment uses count-based incrementing without atomicity guarantees
known_limitations.impact: Two concurrent generate requests could read the same count and produce duplicate versionNumber values
Any email address can be used to create an account without ownership verification

known_limitations.issue: SQLite database used as the datastore
known_limitations.impact: Not suitable for multi-instance deployment or high-concurrency production workloads; limited to single-writer

known_limitations.issue: Bulk mapping update does not verify that all provided mappingIds actually exist
known_limitations.impact: Non-existent mapping IDs are silently ignored; the returned count may be less than the number of IDs provided with no indication of which ones failed

known_limitations.issue: Single mapping PATCH returns 400 when mcpType is already set to the requested value instead of a no-op 200
known_limitations.impact: Clients must track current state to avoid unnecessary error handling for idempotent updates
